Weekend Recap: Kimberly Molto , the author of True Tales of the Paranormal , joined Barbara on the Sunday show. A research scientist specializing in cognitive neurobiology, Molto told of her personal encounters with Psi phenomena which led her to investigate other cases brought to her attention. Ken Alibek the author of Biohazard , was Barbara's guest on Saturday night. Alibek defected to the U.S. in 1992, after directing a bio-weapons research facility in the Soviet Union. He discussed his current efforts to develop new forms of medical protection against biological weapons, as well as his past experiences in the Soviet Union. Messages from Art "Now let me try and answer a few questions," Art Bell said when he returned to the airwaves Friday evening to fill-in for George. Night & Day A bright flash lit up the sky over western Washington state at 2:45 AM on Thursday morning. Pictured above are two shots from a security camera at a Washington casino that show just before and during the flash. After some controversy over the source of the skyburst, a just published AP article confirms that it was a meteor "about the size of a small piece of luggage or a computer monitor." Check out various videos of the flash at these sites: KPTV.com , Seattle Times , and KOMOTV.com . Stardust Project In January 2004, NASA's Stardust spacecraft successfully rendezvoused with comet Wild 2, collecting particles released from its surface and interstellar dust. Sandford, a NASA research astrophysicist, said he hoped the Stardust Project would help scientists determine "what fraction of [cometary material and dust] is organic, what kinds of organics they are and what possible role they may have played in life's emergence on earth." Stardust Project In January 2004, NASA's Stardust spacecraft successfully rendezvoused with comet Wild 2, collecting particles released from its surface and interstellar dust. Sandford, a NASA research astrophysicist, said he hoped the Stardust Project would help scientists determine "what fraction of [cometary material and dust] is organic, what kinds of organics they are and what possible role they may have played in life's emergence on earth."
